location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday

level_signals: Mentoring PMs and elevating how we build

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7–10+ years of product management experience, with a demonstrated track record of leading complex, high-impact product initiatives in high-growth B2B or fintech environments.
Proven ability to define product strategy that translates into measurable business outcomes.
Strong fluency in technical systems and architecture. You are comfortable discussing APIs, data models, experimentation frameworks, and system tradeoffs with engineers.
Highly data-driven. Strong experience defining metrics, running experiments, and using SQL (or similar tools) to guide decisions.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills. Ability to influence senior stakeholders through structured thinking and compelling narratives.
Ability to reason holistically across product, technical, operational, compliance, and revenue systems.
Demonstrated ability to raise the quality bar — for product thinking, execution, and customer experience.
You must be willing to work in the office at least 3 days per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building platform or financial infrastructure products is strongly preferred.

scope.surface: One of Brex's most critical product domains (e.g., financial infrastructure, risk systems, growth systems, payables ecosystem)
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS — corporate cards, banking, spend management, bill pay, and travel software
core_responsibilities: Define and own a multi-quarter product vision and strategy aligned to Brex's highest company priorities.
Identify high-leverage opportunities that create durable competitive advantage, not just incremental improvements.
Lead complex, cross-functional initiatives that span multiple teams, systems, and stakeholder groups.
Develop deep domain expertise in your area (e.g., financial infrastructure, risk systems, growth systems, payables ecosystem, etc.) and use it to inform product direction.
Partner closely with Engineering to design scalable technical systems, engaging meaningfully in architectural discussions and tradeoffs.
Set clear success metrics tied to company-level outcomes and hold teams accountable to measurable impact.
Drive structured decision-making in ambiguous environments, balancing speed with rigor.
Influence senior leaders and executives through clear thinking, strong written narratives, and data-backed recommendations.
Improve product craft and operating standards across the organization, mentoring PMs and elevating how we build.

compensation.equity_form: equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($240K-$300K base)
Clear product surface examples provided (financial infrastructure, risk systems, growth systems, payables ecosystem)
Explicit office days named (Mon/Wed/Thu), reducing ambiguity about hybrid expectations
Strong brand with well-known enterprise customers (DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom, Reddit)
Mentorship responsibility signals meaningful organizational influence for the right candidate
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not stated anywhere in the JD
Hybrid policy requires in-office 3 specific days per week in SF, Seattle, or NYC — fully remote candidates ineligible
Remote work capped at 4 weeks per year with a minimum of one week at a time, which is restrictive
Domain area for this specific role not named (JD gives examples but no single assigned area), making scope unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Staff', comp range, and experience requirements)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'starting base pay will depend on... the candidate's location')
ambiguities: The JD lists a $240K-$300K salary range but notes 'the starting base pay will depend on a number of factors including the candidate's location,' implying possible location-based pay differential, though no explicit tiers are defined.

The product domain for this specific role is not fixed — JD gives examples (financial infrastructure, risk systems, growth systems, payables ecosystem) but does not specify which area this hire will own.
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization requirements anywhere in the JD.
